steps.name: Walking skeleton: section-keyed grouping with custom order
steps.criteria: Composing groupBySection then appendNewSections then sortByCustomOrder over Inner Aisles items at aisles 4 5 7 plus a Produce item with custom order Produce Inner Aisles produces exactly two SectionGroup entries with first.section equal to Produce and second.section equal to Inner Aisles and second.items names in aisle order Bread Pasta Soap.
steps.scenario_name: Carlos opens the store view and sees one card per section with custom order applied

steps.name: Complete groupBySection edge cases and default sort
steps.criteria: groupBySection handles four behavioural edges: (1) within a section containing both numbered and null aisles, numbered items appear before null-aisle items; (2) when sortByCustomOrder receives null custom order, sections appear alphabetically by section name; (3) an empty trip-items array returns an empty SectionGroup array; (4) when every item shares one section with null aisles, exactly one SectionGroup is produced containing every item. All four scenarios in milestone-1-section-keyed-grouping.test.tsx that exercise grouping/sorting domain behaviour must be unskipped and passing.
steps.scenario_name: Null aisle items sort last within a section | No custom order falls back to alphabetical sections | Empty trip yields no section cards | All-null aisle items render in a single section card

steps.name: Narrow appendNewSections to section-name dedupe semantics
steps.criteria: appendNewSections operates on section-name keys only: (1) given an order [Inner Aisles, Deli] and known section names [Inner Aisles, Deli, Sushi Bar], the returned order is [Inner Aisles, Deli, Sushi Bar]; (2) given an order [Inner Aisles, Deli] and known section names [Inner Aisles, Deli] (representing Inner Aisles spanning multiple aisles deduped to one entry), the returned order is unchanged [Inner Aisles, Deli] -- adding a new aisle inside a known section produces zero diff; (3) given an order [Inner Aisles, Deli] and known section names with two new sections, both new section names append in input order. All three US-03 scenarios in milestone-2 must be unskipped and passing.
steps.scenario_name: New section name appends to the order | New aisle inside a known section is a no-op | Multiple new sections append in discovery order

phases.name: Migration, UI swap, and legacy removal

steps.name: Add legacy-composite migration to useSectionOrder on first read
steps.criteria: useSectionOrder, on first mount with a given sectionOrderStorage instance, inspects the loaded order and applies the wipe-on-detect predicate per ADR-004: (1) when the stored order contains any entry whose string includes '::' (e.g. ['Inner Aisles::4', 'Deli::null']), the hook calls clearOrder() and surfaces null; (2) when the stored order contains only section names (e.g. ['Inner Aisles', 'Deli']), storage is left untouched and the hook surfaces the loaded order verbatim; (3) when at least one entry contains '::' alongside clean entries, the hook clears storage; (4) an empty stored order [] is preserved unchanged and the hook surfaces []. All four US-04 scenarios in milestone-2 must be unskipped and passing.
steps.scenario_name: Legacy composite-keyed order is wiped on first read | Already-migrated section-name order is preserved untouched | Mixed legacy and section-name entries are wiped | Empty stored order needs no migration

steps.name: Swap StoreView and SectionOrderSettingsScreen call sites to section-name keys
steps.criteria: StoreView.tsx imports groupBySection (not groupByAisle), builds knownSectionNames from groups.map(g => g.section), passes section names to appendNewSections, and renders one AisleSection per SectionGroup keyed by sectionGroup.section. SectionOrderSettingsScreen.tsx removes parseSectionKey/toSectionKey/formatSectionDisplay, derives knownSectionNames from staple library section names deduped, and renders one row per section name with testIDs of the form section-row-<sectionName>. After this step, rendering SectionOrderSettingsScreen with staples in Inner Aisles aisles 4/5/7 plus Deli (null) produces exactly two rows labelled 'Inner Aisles' and 'Deli', no row contains an aisle-number prefix, and no testID of the form section-row-Inner Aisles::N exists. AisleSection prop is renamed from aisleGroup: AisleGroup to sectionGroup: SectionGroup. Transitional overloads in section-ordering.ts are removed.
steps.scenario_name: Settings screen shows one row per section regardless of aisle count

steps.name: Delete legacy AisleGroup and groupByAisle exports; verify regression suite green
steps.criteria: src/domain/item-grouping.ts no longer exports AisleGroup or groupByAisle (and no longer carries the scaffold marker). The compareAisleGroups helper is renamed/removed in favour of compareSectionGroups (or inlined). The full Jest suite passes, including the prior store-section-order acceptance tests in tests/acceptance/store-section-order/ that exercise the unchanged behaviour for the single-aisle-per-section case (regression safety net per DISTILL [DWD-05]). dependency-cruiser rules continue to pass with no new violations. No imports of AisleGroup or groupByAisle remain in src/.
steps.scenario_name: Regression: prior store-section-order acceptance suite remains green after legacy export removal
